
---
title: "Lexical recommendation engine"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Lexical recommendation engine}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

## Overview

The lexical engine compares five dimensions independently:

1. theme;
2. purpose;
3. method;
4. data;
5. context.

For article `i` and dimension `k`, the dimension score is:

\[
L_{ik} = 0.45\,TFIDF_{ik} + 0.35\,BM25_{ik} + 0.20\,ExactMatch_{ik}.
\]

TF-IDF uses cosine similarity between the normalized query vector and normalized document vectors. BM25 incorporates document frequency and document-length normalization. `ExactMatch` combines predefined multiword concepts with coverage of meaningful query tokens.

## Multidimensional index

The default dimension weights are:

```r
c(
  theme = 0.20,
  method = 0.22,
  data = 0.10,
  context = 0.23,
  purpose = 0.25
)
```

When a field is empty, its weight is set to zero and the remaining weights are divided by their sum. The final index is reported on a 0–100 scale.

## Programmatic use

```r
library(similR)

recommend_articles(
  title = "AI literacy and entrepreneurial intention",
  purpose = "Assess entrepreneurial intention",
  method = "Survey and structural equation model",
  context = "University students in Honduras",
  engine = "lexical",
  n = 20
)
```

## Cache behavior

The first recommendation after installing or updating a database constructs the sparse lexical index. Later searches reuse the in-memory index. New R sessions reuse the disk cache when the database has not changed.
